paper_title: Analytical framework for recurrence-network analysis of time series

methods.description: Process of constructing an ε-recurrence network from a time series, interpreting it as a discrete subnetwork of a continuous graph corresponding to the system's attractor.

steps.description: Interpret an ε-recurrence network as a discrete subnetwork of a continuous graph with uncountably many vertices and edges corresponding to the system’s attractor.
steps.searchable_summary: construct ε-recurrence network from time series data

methods.description: Estimation of various statistical measures for complex network analysis as discrete estimators of continuous measures of geometric properties of the attractor.

steps.description: Show that various statistical measures commonly used in complex network analysis can be seen as discrete estimators of newly defined continuous measures of certain complex geometric properties of the attractor on the scale given by ε.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate statistical measures for network analysis

methods.method_name: Local, Mesoscopic, and Global Measure Introduction
methods.description: Introduction of new local, mesoscopic, and global measures for recurrence networks, providing an objective criterion for the choice of ε.

steps.description: Introduce local measures such as the ε-clustering coefficient, mesoscopic measures such as ε-motif density, path-based measures such as ε-betweennesses, and global measures such as ε-efficiency.
steps.searchable_summary: define new measures for recurrence networks

methods.method_name: Percolation Threshold and Node Splitting Invariant Measures
methods.description: Provide an objective criterion for the choice of ε via a percolation threshold and improve estimation by node splitting invariant versions of the measures.

steps.description: Provide an objective criterion for the choice of ε via a percolation threshold.
Improve estimation by so-called node splitting invariant versions of the measures.

methods.description: Illustrate the framework using archetypical chaotic attractors by deriving analytical expressions for the novel measures and comparing them with data from numerical experiments.

steps.description: Derive analytical expressions for the novel measures for archetypical chaotic attractors such as those of the Bernoulli and logistic maps, periodic and two-dimensional quasi-periodic motions, and for hyperballs and hypercubes.
steps.searchable_summary: derive analytical expressions for chaotic attractors

steps.description: Compare derived analytical expressions with data from numerical experiments.
